launch_contract.title_ticket_policy: NUS title.tmd uses its adjacent title.tik in the read-only ROM tree; Semu never copies or rewrites it.

doc.runtime: Linux launches the exact source-built package from the asset root. -f starts fullscreen, -m selects Semu's isolated MLC, and -g consumes exactly one following Wii U title. The Steam Deck system definition selects file inputs; upstream Cemu additionally understands an extracted title root containing code, content, and meta. No host executable, Flatpak, libretro core, or prebuilt binary participates.
doc.state: Cemu reads settings from $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/Cemu and data from $XDG_DATA_HOME/Cemu. -m pins the writable MLC, including normal title saves under mlc01/usr/save, below ${state_root}/data/Cemu/mlc01. keys.txt is copied only when its isolated target is absent, preferring the bounded user-owned ${semu_home}/firmware/cemu import; an adjacent NUS title.tik remains in the read-only ROM tree. External keys, tickets, ROMs, BIOS material, and existing saves are never modified.
doc.input: controllerProfiles/controller0.xml is Cemu's automatically loaded player-one profile. It pins the Steam Virtual Gamepad through SDL and maps the complete Wii U GamePad surface. Ctrl+Q quits and Ctrl+Tab swaps the selected TV/GamePad view. Upstream Cemu 2.6 has no savestate implementation, so state.save and state.load are declared unsupported in profile.json rather than mapped to inert chords.
doc.rendering: OpenGL is forced through settings.xml and the package build. The direct ABI 2 game phase runs only for the selected main-window TV image after DrawBackbufferQuad; the post-UI phase runs immediately before Cemu's owned swap. The separate pad window never creates a renderer frame.
doc.readiness: A failed -g quick launch exits with status 74 instead of leaving Cemu's deliberately empty quick-launch window blocked behind a modal dialog. Successful readiness still requires a frame-bound shared renderer receipt; process survival alone is never success.
doc.sandbox: The package closure, ROM library, configured BIOS root, and external Cemu data are read-only. Cemu can write only its isolated state root.
